name: Moms Mabley
abstract: Loretta Mary Aiken (March 19, 1897 – May 23, 1975), known by her stage name Moms Mabley, was an American stand-up comedian and actress. Mabley began her career on the theater stage in the 1920s and became a veteran entertainer of the Chitlin' Circuit of black vaudeville.

about_quotes.speaker: Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.evidence: During that time I met, in fact I played for Moms Mabley. Her name was Jackie Mabley then.

about_quotes.speaker: Dorothy Donegan
about_quotes.evidence: I must have done two theatres with them. Jackie “Moms” Mabley, she said, she’d stop the show and say, “Follow that.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Dorothy Donegan — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: The 24th of January, 1947, we went into the Apollo theatre in Harlem which was headlined by the orchestra, Jackie Mabley, the woman comedian...
